scope: TG-P14 close-out: recover the ctx4096 generated-attention promotion miss
answer_summary: The smallest change that recovers the ~0.5pp ctx4096 miss is NOT a new primitive: it is flipping the EXISTING KV_BOTH staging (stage V in 8KB LDS instead of reading V from global during the PV product). Phase 0 attributed the residual to the scalar p*V step reading V from global (K_ONLY); staging V in LDS closes it.

speed.margin_note: ctx4096 worst margin (0.3pp above the 98% floor) ~ run-to-run spread -> MARGINAL, not a robust win

correctness.in_model_prefilled_parity: generated route == owned 48/48 token-identical at ctx>=769 (qk_prefilled_route_parity.py, real prefill, temp=0, deterministic)

vectorized_pv_primitive.what_tried: DECODE_ATTN_BLOCK_TILE_PV_VEC: load the R=4 contiguous V columns as one vec (STACK->wide load), packed FMA av*corr + p*vv over the REG accumulator, distinct-slot REG stores acc[k].store(nv.gep(k)).
vectorized_pv_primitive.block: UOp spec verification failed at the schedule stage: 'Ops.GEP dtypes.float from Ops.ADD dtypes.float.vec(4)' -- gep-extract of a vec4 ALU result stored into the manual-END REG accumulator is not accepted by the current lowering (the same make_floatN manual-accumulator fragility class as TG-P10..P14).
vectorized_pv_primitive.conclusion: the naive STACK-vec + gep-store PV vectorization cannot express the layout through the current UOp lowering; a working version needs the widening/distinct-slot-devec machinery extended to this vec-store-to-REG case -- a deeper codegen effort, out of scope for the 'smallest' change. Reverted; scalar-PV KV_BOTH remains correct.
vectorized_pv_primitive.not_needed_for_task_bar: KV_BOTH already clears the 98% bar; the vec-PV was only pursued for robust margin over the borderline near-tie.

boltbeam_strict_view: boltbeam reopen-check on decode_attention_g5_8b_refuted with the KV_BOTH evidence still returns REFUTE: it treats -1.7% ctx4096 as a protected regression and, as a refuted candidate, requires a WIN over owned to reopen -- not a near-tie. Owned HIP is still ~1.7% faster at ctx4096.

safety_adherence.correctness_authority: prefilled parity + isolated numeric microgate (NOT the unprefilled token gate)
safety_adherence.stopped_at_first_block: vec-PV BLOCKED_PRIMITIVE -> reverted and stopped, did not surgery deeper into fragile codegen

bottom_line: Miss recovered (borderline) with the smallest possible change (existing KV_BOTH staging), correctness rigorously verified, default-off. A decisive owned-beating win needs a vectorized-PV primitive, which is BLOCKED at the UOp-spec level and would require extending the accumulator-widening lowering -- flagged as the durable follow-on.
